message:
Extract interest rate change information from this email.

Email Subject: Important Update: Change in Your Loan Interest Rate- Loan A/C No 644325119
Email Content: HDFC BANK .ExternalClass { width: 100%; } .ExternalClass, .ExternalClass p, .ExternalClass span, .ExternalClass font, .ExternalClass td, .ExternalClass div { line-height: 100%; } body { -webkit-text-size-adjust: none; -ms-text-size-adjust: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; } table { border-spacing: 0; } table td { border-collapse: collapse; } body, #body_style { color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.4; } body { *width: 600px; margin: 0 auto; width: 600px\9; } @media only screen and (min-width: 600px) { body { margin: 0 auto; width: 600px; } .widthTable { padding-left: 30px; } } @media only screen and (max-width: 480px) { body, table { width: 100% !important; } .font320 { font-size: 0.9em !important; } .font3201 { font-size: 0.8em !important; } .cta { white-space: nowrap; } .widthTable { padding-left: 30px; } } @media only screen and (max-width: 599px) { /* TEMPORARY */ body[yahoo] .hide { display: none !important; } body[yahoo] .table { width: 100% !important; } .widthTable { padding-left: 30px; } table { border-collapse: collapse; mso-table-lspace: 0pt; mso-table-rspace: 0pt; } } /*** END TEMPORARY ***/ Important Update: Change in Your Loan Interest Rate Dear Customer, Greetings from HDFC Bank! We hope you're doing well. We have great news for you! Due to the recent revision in the Repo Rate by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Dec 5, 2025 your loan interest rate will reduce by 0.25% starting from your next interest reset date. Please visit our portal www.hdfc.bank.in to know about your next rate reset date. What This Means for You Lower Interest Rate: Your loan will now have a reduced interest rate. Effective from Your Reset Date: The new rate will apply automatically on your scheduled interest reset date. Need Help? Check Your Loan Details: Log in to your HDFC Online Account Use the HDFC App: Download on Apple Download on Google Play For any further assistance, please feel free to reach out. Warm regards, HDFC Bank Ltd. Titash_24-02-25

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. accountNumber: Loan account number (e.g., "644325119", "A/C No: 123456", "Loan A/C No 644325119")
   - Extract from various formats: "A/C No:", "Account No:", "Loan A/C No", "Loan Account Number"
   - May be partially masked (e.g., "XX4325119")

2. loanName: Loan name/description (e.g., "Property Loan", "Home Loan", "House Loan")

3. oldRate: Previous interest rate as a NUMBER (e.g., 7.45 for 7.45%, 8.25 for 8.25%)
   - Extract the OLD/PREVIOUS rate that is being changed FROM
   - Look for phrases like "from 7.45%", "previous rate", "current rate", "existing rate", "earlier rate"
   - The rate can be INCREASED or DECREASED - extract the rate that was BEFORE the change
   - Must be a number (e.g., 7.45, 8.25, 9.0)

4. newRate: New interest rate as a NUMBER (e.g., 8.25 for 8.25%, 7.45 for 7.45%)
   - Extract the NEW rate that is being changed TO
   - Look for phrases like "to 8.25%", "new rate", "revised rate", "updated rate", "revised to"
   - The rate can be HIGHER or LOWER than the old rate (both increases and decreases are valid)
   - Must be a number (e.g., 8.25, 7.45, 9.0, 10.5)
   - CRITICAL: Extract the EXACT rate value, including decimals (e.g., 8.25 not 8.2 or 8, 7.45 not 7.4 or 7)
   - IMPORTANT: If email says "rate reduced" or "rate decreased", the newRate will be LOWER than oldRate
   - IMPORTANT: If email says "rate increased" or "rate revised upward", the newRate will be HIGHER than oldRate

5. effectiveDate: Effective date of rate change in YYYY-MM-DD format
   - Look for "effective from", "effective date", "with effect from", "w.e.f.", "effective"
   - Parse dates in various formats (DD-MM-YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, Month DD YYYY, etc.)
   - Convert to YYYY-MM-DD format

6. confidence: Your confidence in the extraction (0-1)

EXAMPLES:
- If email says "Interest rate changed from 7.45% to 8.25%", extract: oldRate: 7.45, newRate: 8.25
- If email says "Interest rate reduced from 8.25% to 7.45%", extract: oldRate: 8.25, newRate: 7.45
- If email says "New rate: 8.25% p.a. (previous: 7.45%)", extract: oldRate: 7.45, newRate: 8.25
- If email says "Rate reduced to 7.45% from 8.25%", extract: oldRate: 8.25, newRate: 7.45
- If email says "Rate updated to 8.25%", you may need to infer oldRate from context or use 0 if not mentioned

Return ONLY a valid JSON object with the extracted data.
systemPrompt: You are an expert at extracting interest rate change data from emails. You must return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown, no explanations, just JSON. The JSON must include: accountNumber (string), loanName (string), oldRate (number), newRate (number), effectiveDate (YYYY-MM-DD), confidence (number 0-1).
